body:
I built on-device 3d gen for iphone

the part that surprised me wasn't the speed (>100s) or the package size (~7mb + runtime model download)

it was that ram drops from ~4gb to 1.9gb after model load

leaving plenty of headroom for the rest of the app

Been seeing everyone hype up app mascots and animations on here.   

Sooo...  

Let me introduce you to Greg. Our new mascot for Goblin Mode👹

Are apps just slowly becoming restaurant meals?

People can vibecode at home, but sometimes it's worth the money and time savings to download an app made by someone else?

Spent a few hours rebuilding my app's onboarding from a stack of forms into a narrated video flow.

The goal is to make the onboarding process fun for the user!

Most “AI image apps” are just screenshots traveling to a server.

I wanted:
 • weights on device
 • generation on device
 • images never leaving the phone

So I spent the last week turning sd.cpp into an actual iOS developer experience.

SwiftPM install. One simple generate()

Just finished building an AI browser agent that runs entirely on your iPhone.

You can:
• tell it what you need in plain English
• watch it tap, scroll, and read pages for you
• run unlimited tasks for $0

No cloud. No API keys. All on an iPhone.

I built a SwiftUI compiler that runs entirely on-device and is only a 6mb package

You can:
• write SwiftUI code
• have AI generate views locally
• hit run and see it instantly

No Mac. No Xcode. All on an iPhone.

I trained a model to guess calories from food pics.

It beats GPT-4o, Claude, and Gemini. Runs locally with only 2GB RAM

Average error under 51 calories. Here's what I learned 👇

I jailbroke my Mac's Neural Engine.

Not the GPU. Not the CPU. The AI chip Apple hides behind Siri and photo blur.

15 trillion operations per second. Sitting idle on every MacBook sold since 2020.

I made it run a full LLM. Here's how 🧵
